In other words, proponents of Living Constitutionalism believe that portions of the Constitution must change in meaning from time to time. The idea is that the mores of modern society should be taken into account when interpreting constitutional phrases. The term Living Constitution appears to have first been used in the title of a 1927 book of that name by Profesor Howard McBain. But, the roots of the idea that have grown and survived belong to others long before the late Professor, and with all due respect, much more well recognized.
